description: Remove the SDK version mismatch badge from PluginCard. The badge shows when a plugin's tools.json lacks an sdkVersion field, which only happens with hand-crafted or ancient plugins that predate the SDK toolchain writing this field. No real user will ever see it. The sdkVersion field stays in the protocol (useful for diagnostics) — only the UI badge is removed.

userStories.title: Remove SDK badge from PluginCard
userStories.description: Remove the SDK version mismatch badge and its Tooltip wrapper from PluginCard.tsx. The sdkVersion field remains in the PluginState type and protocol — only the visual badge is removed.
acceptanceCriteria: The SDK Badge and its Tooltip wrapper are removed from PluginCard.tsx
No SDK badge appears for any plugin regardless of sdkVersion value
The sdkVersion field is still passed through the protocol (no changes to bridge, background-message-handlers, message-router, or extension-protocol)
Storybook stories still work (sdkVersion in mock data is harmless — just unused by the UI now)
Build passes, lint passes, knip passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (remove badge)

Context:
PluginCard.tsx lines 184-196:
```tsx
{!plugin.sdkVersion && (
  <Tooltip>
    <Tooltip.Trigger asChild>
      <Badge
        variant="outline"
        size="sm"
        className="border-accent bg-accent/10 align-middle text-accent-foreground">
        SDK
      </Badge>
    </Tooltip.Trigger>
    <Tooltip.Content>SDK version mismatch — rebuild plugin</Tooltip.Content>
  </Tooltip>
)}
```

Changes:
Delete the entire block (lines 184-196). The line before it (line 183, closing `)}` of the DEV badge) and the line after it (line 197, `{!plugin.reviewed && (`) remain untouched.

After removal, check if `Badge` is still imported — it is used by the DEV badge at line 180, so the import stays.

Check if `Tooltip` is still imported — it is used by the version tooltip (line 174) and the review shield icon (line 198), so the import stays.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the `sdkVersion` field from the type definition, protocol, message router, or background handlers. It is still useful in `/health` endpoint diagnostics.
- Do NOT modify Storybook stories — `sdkVersion: '0.0.3'` in mock data is harmless.
- Do NOT modify bridge.test.ts — `sdkVersion` in the mock PluginState is harmless.
- Do NOT change the loader.ts warning log for missing sdkVersion — that still has diagnostic value on the server side.
